family_context.preferred_cover_geography: Georgia and Germany, ideally worldwide excluding the US

method_notes: This AC 50102 artifact records, per candidate, whether public terms/conditions, policy wording, or benefit schedules were accessible from reviewed public sources during this run.
Statuses are intentionally granular: brochure-only and generic-website-terms-only are kept distinct from policy wording, benefit schedules, or fully missing/blocked materials.
A record can show public brochure or plan-description access without proving that policy wording or a table of benefits was publicly accessible.
Generic website legal terms were not treated as plan-specific wording unless the reviewed evidence tied them directly to the insurance product.
When reviewed evidence exposed only template placeholders, blocked responses, or generic legal PDFs, the uncertainty is preserved explicitly rather than upgraded to verified plan-material access.

records.plan_name: International Health Insurance / Global Medical Cover

evidence_citations.page_reference: reviewed public landing/quote/broker page checked for plan-document access

uncertainty_notes: No clearly plan-specific downloadable brochure/PDF was found in the reviewed public sources during this run; a gated quote flow, alternate locale, or deeper broker/application path may still expose one later.
Only a corporate modern-slavery PDF appeared in the reviewed public page scan; no plan terms/conditions, policy wording, or benefit schedule was publicly verified.

evidence_citations.page_reference: reviewed public landing/quote/broker page checked for plan-document access

uncertainty_notes: No clearly plan-specific downloadable brochure/PDF was found in the reviewed public sources during this run; a gated quote flow, alternate locale, or deeper broker/application path may still expose one later.
At least one reviewed public page returned HTTP 403 in this environment, so missing-document status may reflect access controls rather than true absence.
Official landing page returned HTTP 403 in this environment and the quote path showed no PDFs; missing-material status may reflect access controls.
